roadmap.goal: Adopt the Entity Supertype (Table-per-Type) data model for graph nodes; scope every node to a project; introduce a unified relationships edge table; consolidate validation on drizzle-zod; and group volatile graph features under a single WorkspaceModule umbrella to eliminate circular-dependency churn.

phases.name: Schema
phases.description: Define all Drizzle table files in the new database/schema/ directory, replacing today's single schema.ts. Covers the Entity Supertype base table, all subtypes, the unified edge table, shared auth tables, and the barrel + migration regeneration.

steps.name: entities + project_meta schema files
steps.description: Create entities.schema.ts (supertype registry with self-referential project_id FK) and project-meta.schema.ts (project subtype with owner_id FK to users). These two files are the foundation every other schema file imports.
criteria: entities table compiles with a working self-referential project_id FK using onDelete cascade
project_meta.id is simultaneously the PK and a FK to entities.id (Table-per-Type pattern)
entityType pgEnum with values project/thought/label is exported for downstream reuse
index on entities.project_id and index on project_meta.owner_id are declared
npm run build succeeds from apps/api (temporary direct imports acceptable until barrel lands in step 01-05)
steps.implementation_notes:
NEW files — no prior equivalent. Use Drizzle's self-reference pattern: references((): any => entities.id, { onDelete: 'cascade' }). A minimal users stub (id only) in users.schema.ts is acceptable here; task 04 fleshes it out. Do NOT create the barrel index.ts yet — that belongs to step 01-05. Export entityType pgEnum so validators and services can import it without re-declaring.

For the schema compilation check, a single-example integration test that drizzle() boots with these tables is appropriate; property tests apply at the service layer in later steps.
steps.scenario_name: entities self-referential project_id and project_meta TPT key

steps.name: thoughts + labels subtype schema files
steps.description: Create thought.schema.ts and label.schema.ts. Both tables use their id as a FK to entities.id (Table-per-Type). Thoughts drop user_id, project_id, parent_id, is_root. Color is inline varchar(7) on both tables — never a FK.
criteria: thoughts table compiles with no user_id, project_id, parent_id, or is_root columns
labels table compiles with no user_id or project_id columns
thoughts.color and labels.color are both inline varchar(7) nullable/defaulted respectively
Both tables declare id as PK and FK to entities.id with onDelete cascade
relations() tying each subtype id back to entities (one-to-one) are declared
steps.implementation_notes:
REPLACE — reference current src/database/schema.ts for field parity on body/title/content_hash/canvas geometry, but drop the listed columns. The colors table is being dropped in step 01-04; never create a color FK here. No parent_id — feed hierarchy will live in relationships (step 01-03). index on content_hash is recommended for dedup parity with idx_thoughts_content_hash.

steps.scenario_name: thoughts and labels subtype FK and stripped columns

steps.name: relationships unified edge table schema file
steps.description: Create relationship.schema.ts defining the single edge table discriminated by a kind enum (hierarchy/tag/edge). Includes all four indexes and the three partial unique constraints that enforce per-kind invariants at the DB layer.
criteria: relationships table compiles with kind as NOT NULL enum and label_id as nullable FK to entities.id with onDelete set null
Partial unique index UNIQUE (source_id) WHERE kind='hierarchy' exists
Partial unique index UNIQUE (source_id, target_id) WHERE kind='tag' exists
Partial unique index UNIQUE (source_id, target_id, label_id) WHERE kind='edge' exists
relationshipKind pgEnum is exported; direction-convention comment is present in the file
steps.implementation_notes:
NEW file. Use Drizzle's uniqueIndex(...).where(sql`...`) for the three partial unique constraints — do not skip them; they are the DB-level enforcement of the per-kind invariants that the service layer (step 03-03) relies on. All four FKs (project_id, source_id, target_id, label_id) reference entities.id with their respective onDelete policies. Document the direction-convention table in a file-level comment: hierarchy = child→parent, tag = thought→label, edge = node→node. Endpoint-type validation (tag target must be a label) is service-layer only — not a DB constraint.

criteria: chunks table has project_id FK to entities.id with cascade and no user_id column
chunks table carries the working vector(768) custom pgvector type copied from current schema.ts
users, credentials, mcp_auth_codes, mcp_refresh_tokens are present and unchanged in shape
No colors table and no thought_labels table exist in database/schema/
Files compile alongside steps 01-01 through 01-03

REPLACE chunks (add project_id, drop user_id); KEEP users/credentials/mcp-tokens verbatim. Copy the customType vector(768) definition exactly from the current src/database/schema.ts — do not reinvent it. If step 01-01 left only a users stub, flesh it out to the full definition now. Drop colors: color is an inline varchar(7) on thoughts and labels (steps 01-01/01-02); normalizing behind a UUID FK costs a join for 7 bytes. Drop thought_labels: tagging is kind='tag' in relationships (step 01-03).

steps.name: Schema barrel, drizzle.config repoint, and migration regeneration
steps.description: Assemble database/schema/index.ts re-exporting all tables and relations, update drizzle.config.ts to point at the directory, delete the old schema.ts and obsolete migration scripts, then generate and apply a single fresh migration against an empty database.
criteria: npm run build succeeds from apps/api with no references to the deleted schema.ts
A single fresh migration generates and applies to an empty database without error
DatabaseService boots and exposes db typed with the full schema (all ten tables present)
No references remain to deleted files: schema.ts, run-migration-colors.js, run-migration-is-edge.js, run-migration-scoping.js, apply-migration-0005.mjs
drizzle-kit generate produces no unexpected diff against the applied schema
steps.implementation_notes:
REPLACE single schema.ts with schema/ directory barrel. The DatabaseService import line 'import * as schema from ./schema' should resolve to the barrel without modification — verify before assuming a change is needed. Delete old migration SQL + meta/ directory (no live data; destructive is acceptable). Delete the four obsolete scripts listed in acceptance criteria; keep test-oauth-flow.mjs. The generated migration must include CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector; if drizzle-kit does not emit it, add it manually or document it as a pre-step. Run npx drizzle-kit generate then npx drizzle-kit migrate against a fresh/empty DB and confirm clean apply.

EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: structural scaffolding (barrel assembly, config repoint, migration file generation) with no domain logic. Compensate with a single-example integration test at this step that boots DatabaseService and queries each table name from information_schema to confirm the full schema landed.
steps.scenario_name: full schema applies to empty database and DatabaseService boots

phases.name: Core Modules
phases.description: Stand up UsersModule (profile CRUD split from AuthModule), ProjectsModule (project lifecycle + the create-project transaction + the exported ownership gate), and WorkspaceModule scaffold (empty umbrella with six sub-folders).

steps.name: UsersModule (profile CRUD split from AuthModule)
steps.description: Create apps/api/src/users/ with UsersService as the single place that creates/reads/updates users rows. AuthModule imports UsersModule and delegates profile lookups to it. Credential/session/token logic stays in AuthModule.
criteria: npm run build succeeds with UsersModule registered and AuthModule importing it
Auth flows still resolve a user with no behavioral regression in login or JWT issuance
UsersService is the single place that creates, reads, and updates users rows
credentials, mcp_auth_codes, and mcp_refresh_tokens handling remains in AuthModule and is not moved here
steps.implementation_notes:
NEW module. Audit apps/api/src/auth/auth.service.ts to identify which methods touch the users table directly and move those to UsersService. Leave token/credential/session logic in place. A users.controller.ts is optional — only add profile endpoints if the web app needs them; document the choice. UsersService must be exported from UsersModule so AuthModule and ProjectsModule (step 02-02) can inject it.

steps.name: ProjectsModule (create-project transaction + ownership gate)
steps.description: Create apps/api/src/projects/ implementing the project lifecycle: create (atomic two-row transaction: entities root + project_meta), list, update, delete. Export assertOwnership() — the single tenant-boundary check reused by all downstream workspace services.
criteria: Creating a project yields exactly one entities row (type=project, project_id=self) and one project_meta row; failure of either rolls back both
assertOwnership is exported and throws ForbiddenException when owner_id does not match the requesting user
Deleting a project cascades to all its child entities, relationships, and chunks via the self-referential FK
npm run build succeeds with JwtAuthGuard protecting all controller endpoints
steps.implementation_notes:
NEW module. Use db.transaction(...) for the create-project two-step: generate uuid X up front, INSERT entities (id=X, project_id=X, type='project') then INSERT project_meta (id=X, owner_id=caller, ...). The self-referential cascade on entities means delete-project is simply deleting the entities row — no manual cleanup needed. assertOwnership is the tenant boundary defined in §2.3: SELECT owner_id FROM project_meta WHERE id = projectId; throw if missing or mismatch. Export it so workspace services can import it without circular deps.

TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests MUST be written as property-based tests using Hypothesis @given strategies + state-delta matchers (assert_state_delta over a declared universe with strict=True). Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a # bypass: comment if used. steps.name: WorkspaceModule scaffold (empty umbrella + sub-folders)
steps.description: Create apps/api/src/workspace/workspace.module.ts and the six empty sub-folders (thoughts/, labels/, relationships/, pipeline/, gateway/, validation/) so subsequent tasks have a known home. Register WorkspaceModule in app.module.ts.
criteria: npm run build succeeds with WorkspaceModule registered in app.module.ts
All six sub-folders exist: thoughts, labels, relationships, pipeline, gateway, validation
workspace.module.ts imports DatabaseModule and ProjectsModule with TODO markers for forthcoming providers
No domain logic is added — this step is structural scaffolding only
steps.implementation_notes:
NEW module shell. Rationale for the umbrella: thoughts/labels/relationships/chunks reference each other constantly; bundling them avoids cross-module circular-import friction while staying decoupled at the real seams (auth, projects, users, database). Old modules (ThoughtsModule, LabelsModule, ChunkingModule, EmbeddingModule, ColorsModule, McpEventsModule) will be removed in their respective tasks — leave them in app.module.ts imports for now if removing them breaks the build; note the intended end state in a comment.

EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: structural scaffolding with no domain logic. Compensate with property-based tests at the first step that adds behaviour (step 03-01).
steps.scenario_name: WorkspaceModule registers and six sub-folders exist

phases.name: Workspace Domain
phases.description: Implement the four workspace sub-services: thoughts CRUD (entity+subtype transaction), labels CRUD, relationships service (all three kinds with per-kind validation and recursive hierarchy CTE), and pipeline relocation (chunking + embedding adapted to project_id scoping).

steps.name: Workspace thoughts service + controller
steps.description: Reimplement thought CRUD under workspace/thoughts/. A thought is an entities row (type=thought) + a thoughts row, created together in a transaction and ownership-gated via assertOwnership. Absorbs the dropped ColorsModule (color is inline on the thoughts row).
criteria: Creating a thought writes paired entities and thoughts rows in one transaction; failure rolls back both
All reads filter by entities.project_id and are gated by assertOwnership before any DB access
Color set and clear update thoughts.color inline with no reference to the dropped colors table
Delete removes the entities row and cascades to the thoughts row, its chunks, and any relationships referencing it
steps.implementation_notes:
REPLACE existing thoughts module (port from apps/api/src/thoughts/ but adapt). Do NOT reintroduce parent_id — feed hierarchy is a kind='hierarchy' relationship (step 03-03). Leave input validation as light DTOs for now; step 04-01 swaps in drizzle-zod. Leave clearly marked TODO hooks where mutations would publish a real-time event (step 04-02 slots in there). Color set/clear: UPDATE thoughts SET color = ? WHERE id = ? — no FK, no colors table lookup.

steps.name: Workspace labels service + controller
steps.description: Reimplement label CRUD under workspace/labels/. A label is an entities row (type=label) + a labels row in one transaction. Tagging (assign/unassign label to thought) is explicitly NOT here — it lives in the relationships service as kind='tag'.
criteria: Label create writes paired entities and labels rows in one transaction; failure rolls back both
findByProject filters by entities.project_id and calls assertOwnership before any DB access
No assignLabel, unassignLabel, or thought_labels logic exists in the labels service or controller
Delete removes the entities row, cascading to the labels row and nullifying label_id on any relationships

KEEP logic, REPLACE location (port from apps/api/src/labels/ but adapt to subtype schema). Key gotcha: any existing assignLabel/unassignLabel methods move to step 03-03 as kind='tag' operations — do not recreate them here. Leave validation light; leave TODO emit hooks for step 04-02.

steps.name: Workspace relationships service + controller
steps.description: Implement the service over the unified relationships table for all three kinds: hierarchy (feed tree with recursive CTE), tag (thought-label assignment), and edge (canvas connections). Enforces cross-project invariant, per-kind endpoint-type rules, and surfaces clean errors on partial-unique conflicts.
criteria: Each kind enforces its endpoint-type rule: hierarchy requires thought/thought, tag requires thought/label, edge allows any nodes
Cross-project invariant rejects any relationship where source, target, or label_id entities have differing project_id values
Duplicate tag, duplicate edge, and second-parent writes are rejected with a clean error (not a raw DB exception)
A recursive-CTE children/descendants query exists and returns the correct feed tree for hierarchy rows
steps.implementation_notes:
NEW service. Common precondition for every write: assertOwnership then load source/target entities and assert their project_id all equal the request projectId. For hierarchy: DB partial unique (source_id) WHERE kind='hierarchy' enforces one-parent; catch the unique-constraint error and surface a 409 or descriptive message. For the recursive-CTE feed: WITH RECURSIVE over kind='hierarchy' rows — see §2.2 direction conventions (source=child, target=parent). Leave TODO emit hooks for step 04-02.

steps.name: Workspace pipeline relocation (chunking + embedding)
steps.description: Move apps/api/src/chunking/ and apps/api/src/embedding/ into workspace/pipeline/ and adapt chunk persistence to use project_id instead of user_id. Semantic search (remember) and elaborate read paths are updated to scope by project_id and gate by ownership.

Creating or editing a thought body still triggers re-chunking and re-embedding (async/background behaviour preserved)
Semantic search returns results scoped to the owning project and ownership-gated
npm run build succeeds with pipeline providers registered in WorkspaceModule

Keep vector(768) type usage identical to step 01-04's schema definition. Remove old top-level ChunkingModule / EmbeddingModule if the build stays green after the move; otherwise defer to step 05-02 (final cleanup).

phases.name: Quality and Real-time
phases.description: Replace class-validator DTOs with drizzle-zod schemas and a ZodValidationPipe (layer 1 intrinsic validation only), then generalize the existing MCP-only SSE bus into a workspace gateway that broadcasts every mutation regardless of source.

steps.name: Validation: drizzle-zod schemas + ZodValidationPipe
steps.description: Create workspace/validation/ with request schemas built via createInsertSchema() + .pick/.omit/.extend/.refine for thoughts, labels, and relationships. Wire a ZodValidationPipe to all new controllers. Remove class-validator DTO files.
criteria: Controllers validate via drizzle-zod schemas and invalid payloads receive a clean 400 response
color fields on create-thought and create-label are validated against /^#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}$/ when present
Relationship input schema rejects source_id === target_id via .refine() and constrains label_id to kind='edge' only
No class-validator DTO files remain under apps/api/src/thoughts/dto/ or apps/api/src/labels/dto/
Schemas are exported from workspace/validation/ for the internal-mcp layer to import in step 05-01
steps.implementation_notes:
REPLACE class-validator DTOs. Layer 1 only — no async/stateful refinements in Zod (no DB lookups for ownership, existence, or uniqueness; those belong in services). The create-thought request schema is composed from entities + thoughts inferred schemas (spans two tables per §3) — do not use a raw single-table insert schema. Add drizzle-zod to apps/api package.json. Drop class-validator/class-transformer usage where it was only serving these DTOs; keep global ValidationPipe config only if still needed for other parts of the codebase.

steps.name: Workspace gateway (real-time SSE); remove mcp-events
steps.description: Create workspace/gateway/workspace.gateway.ts generalizing the existing per-user RxJS Subject SSE bus. Move event publishers into thoughts/labels/relationships services so every mutation broadcasts a typed WorkspaceEvent with source: user|mcp. Remove McpEventsModule.
criteria: A mutation through thoughts, labels, or relationships service publishes exactly one WorkspaceEvent with the correct source field
The per-user SSE endpoint streams only that user's events with heartbeat intact
mcp-events module, service, and controller are deleted with no remaining imports in app.module.ts
npm run build succeeds with no references to McpEventsService or McpEventsModule
steps.implementation_notes:
REPLACE mcp-events. Base the new gateway on apps/api/src/mcp-events/mcp-events.service.ts (per-user Subject, heartbeat, SSE Observable<SseMessage>) — the pattern works well. Extend the event payload with source: 'user' | 'mcp' and keep entity type + resource ids as WorkspaceEvent. Publishers fill in the TODO hooks left in steps 03-01/03-02/03-03. Do NOT introduce WebSockets — SSE + REST writes is the chosen design per §5. The internal-mcp controller's emit calls are removed here; when source='mcp' is needed, pass a source flag through the service call or set it based on call context. Keep event shape close to current McpToolEvent to minimize frontend churn.

phases.name: Integration and Cleanup
phases.description: Rewire internal-mcp controllers to new services (projects/workspace) while keeping the sidecar HTTP contract unchanged, then perform final app.module.ts wiring, delete all dropped modules/directories, and verify the full build + migration + smoke-test pipeline.

steps.name: Rewire internal-mcp controllers to new services
steps.description: Keep the /api/internal/mcp/* HTTP contract intact for the apps/mcp sidecar, but point every handler at ProjectsService, ThoughtsService, LabelsService, RelationshipsService, and the pipeline services. Remove emit calls (gateway now publishes). Apply drizzle-zod schemas to validate incoming payloads.
criteria: Every /api/internal/mcp/* endpoint delegates to a new service with no references to ColorsService, thought_labels, root-thought project creation, or McpEventsService
add-label-to-thought and remove-label-from-thought delegate to RelationshipsService as kind=tag operations
set-thought-color and clear-thought-color delegate to ThoughtsService inline color update
MCP tool tests in apps/mcp/src/tools/*.test.ts pass end-to-end against the rewired endpoints
apps/mcp sidecar and its api-client.ts are untouched
steps.implementation_notes:
KEEP (rewired). McpInternalGuard and x-mcp-user-id header handling are preserved — do not touch them. list-projects / create-project → ProjectsService (projects are type='project' entities now, not root thoughts). list-thoughts filtering by project uses project_id. thought-to-prompt assembles parent/children via RelationshipsService hierarchy queries and tags via kind='tag'. Remove all emit(...) calls from the controller — the workspace services now publish events (step 04-02). Apply the shared drizzle-zod schemas exported in step 04-01 so MCP and UI obey identical validation rules.

steps.name: app.module wiring, dead module deletion, and full verify
steps.description: Update app.module.ts to the final six-module import set, delete all dropped/relocated directories, grep for every stale reference, and run all five verification steps: build, lint, schema diff, fresh-DB smoke test, and MCP tool tests.
criteria: app.module.ts imports exactly DatabaseModule, AuthModule, UsersModule, ProjectsModule, WorkspaceModule, InternalMcpModule and no others
Deleted directories apps/api/src/thoughts, labels, chunking, embedding, colors, mcp-events leave no dangling imports
Grep for is_root, parent_id, thought_labels, colors, ColorsService, McpEventsService, and the old database/schema.ts path returns zero matches outside planning docs
Full smoke test passes: create user, create project, create thought with color, tag it, create an edge, semantic search, delete project verifies cascade leaves no orphan entities/relationships/chunks
MCP tool tests in apps/mcp pass against the running api
steps.implementation_notes:
KEEP (rewired final state). This is the integration verification step — no new logic. Verification sequence: (1) npm run build from apps/api; (2) npm run lint (no new errors); (3) npx drizzle-kit generate produces no unexpected diff; (4) migrate fresh DB and run the smoke-test flow; (5) run apps/mcp tool tests against the running api. The target directory tree from §7 of the architecture spec is the ground truth — verify folder-for-folder. If any prior step left a TODO that was deferred to this step, resolve it now.

TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests MUST be written as property-based tests using Hypothesis @given strategies + state-delta matchers (assert_state_delta over a declared universe with strict=True). Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a # bypass: comment if used. Acceptance scenarios MUST be framed as Property: blocks (quantified preconditions + invariant outcomes) where the contract supports it; classic Scenario: is fallback. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants.

STAGE CONTEXT: NEW code = Stage 0 (paradigm-from-day-zero, no debt accumulates).
steps.scenario_name: end-to-end flow: project create through cascade delete with no orphans
